item.title: Bessent Says US Will Extract Funds From Iran Accounts If Needed
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US will tap into frozen Iranian assets to pay for any damage the country inflicts on its allies in the Gulf.

item.title: Evercore Sees ‘Wild-Eyed’ FOMO Still Ahead as SpaceX Debut Looms
item.description: Stock investors are discounting the types of events that usually rattle Wall Street as fear of missing out on future gains grips the market on the eve of SpaceX’s trading debut, Evercore ISI’s Julian Emanuel said.

item.title: US Workers’ Health Insurance Costs Set to Rise, Survey Finds
item.description: Employers say higher prices are forcing them to raise premiums. Some will also stop covering GLP-1s.

item.title: DoorDash Built an AI Chatbot to Help With Orders, Reservations and Grocery Lists
item.description: DoorDash Inc. introduced an in-app artificial intelligence chatbot to help customers make restaurant and grocery orders, and suggested the technology could also be used to unlock new enterprise revenue streams.

item.title: Amazon Says Its Data Centers Use 2.5 Billion Gallons of Water
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. said its data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water worldwide last year, or about 5% of the amount metro Seattle consumes annually.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Draws More Than $70 Billion in Retail Orders
item.description: SpaceX’s initial public offering has attracted more than $70 billion in orders from retail investors, according to people familiar with the matter, as the potentially record-breaking debut enters the home stretch.

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s ADIC Plans $15 Billion Leveraged Hedge Fund Bet
item.description: An Abu Dhabi-based investor is considering building exposure worth $15 billion with global hedge funds, people familiar with the matter said, an outlay that will mark one of the emirate’s most significant forays into the sector.

item.title: UK Energy Regulator Warns Data Centers Are Hoarding Grid Capacity
item.description: The UK’s energy regulator is concerned that data centers are reserving large amounts of grid capacity years before fully needed, further threatening the efficiency of networks facing long queues for new supply.

item.title: Odd Lots Is Taking Over Hong Kong (Or the Newsletter, At Least)
item.description: China markets, bowls of noodles, and more.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Jump to 229,000, Highest Since February
item.description: US initial jobless claims unexpectedly rose to the highest since February, potentially reflecting the usual volatility around school summer breaks and holidays.

item.title: US Producer Prices Rise at Fastest Pace Since November 2022
item.description: US producer prices rose in May at the fastest pace in more than three years as the fallout from the Iran war continued to fan inflation pressures.

item.title: Citigroup Offers Its First Investment-Grade Bonds of 2026
item.description: Citigroup Inc. is offering its first investment-grade bonds of 2026, following $123.3 billion of issuance by the five other biggest Wall Street banks this year.

item.title: ECB Hikes for First Time Since 2023 as Inflation Heats Up
item.description: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in almost three years, concluding it can no longer wait out the Iran war as inflation pressures intensify.

item.title: Iran’s Oil Production Slumped 19% Last Month, OPEC Data Show
item.description: Iran’s crude oil production slumped by 19% last month, according to data from OPEC, while the US blockaded the country’s ports during their ongoing conflict.

item.title: US Nuclear Power’s Technological ‘Rebirth’ Will Take Some Time
item.description: The first small-reactor startup in a government program reaches the milestone of a self-sustaining reaction.

item.title: Apollo Is Screening All Software Investments for AI Threat Risk
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. is assessing every new investment opportunity in the software industry for AI disruption risk, as asset managers try to quell investor concerns that rapid advances in the technology could make businesses obsolete.

item.title: Base10, Black-Led VC Firm, Boosts Managed Assets to $2.6 Billion
item.description: Base10 Partners, one of the biggest Black-led venture capital firms, has closed two funds and boosted assets under management to $2.6 billion.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 11, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.7% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York as the latest US strikes against Iran came to a swift end.

item.title: Retail Traders Dump Big Tech to Raise ‘Dry Powder’ to Buy SpaceX
item.description: Retail traders’ infatuation with high-flying computer memory and storage companies is fading now that the next big thing — SpaceX’s initial public offering — is on the way.

item.title: Anduril CEO Says He’s Open to Building Weapons Hub Outside of US
item.description: Anduril Industries Inc., one of the world’s most valuable defense-tech startups, would “absolutely” consider building its next weapons manufacturing hub outside the US, said Chief Executive Officer Brian Schimpf.

item.title: Data Center Battery Maker ZincFive to Merge With SparkLabs SPAC
item.description: ZincFive Inc., a specialized battery maker supplying data centers, has agreed to go public through a merger with a blank-check company backed by SparkLabs Group at a pre-money valuation of $600 million.

item.title: Software Stock Reversal Has Traders Bracing for More Pain Ahead
item.description: A recent rally in software stocks is proving to be short-lived, and investors are bracing for more selling as fears linger about artificial intelligence disruption.

item.title: Lloyds Plans SRT Tied to $4.2 Billion of Loans to Smaller Firms
item.description: Lloyds Banking Group Plc is looking to sell its first significant risk transfer deal of the year, linked to loans to small and medium-sized companies, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Why Doesn’t China Have a Lionel Messi?
item.description: Bloomberg Economics examines why the ‘China Model’ hasn’t created success on the football pitch.

item.title: ShopMy Turns Everyone Into an Influencer Seeking a Commission
item.description: The site raises questions about the motives behind product recommendations

item.title: Turkey Extends Rates Pause Despite Grim Inflation Outlook
item.description: Turkey’s central bank kept its main interest rate unchanged for a third consecutive time since the outbreak of the Iran war on the back of a cooling economy and subdued demand for hard currency.

item.title: World Cup Kicks Off Logistics Scramble Across North American Continent
item.description: A 5,000-vehicles fleet will shuttle a million pounds of team equipment to 104 matches.

item.title: ECB Set to Raise Interest Rates in First Hike Since 2023
South African Business Body Questions Rate Hike as More Seen
item.description: South Africa’s main industry body questioned the central bank’s decision to raise interest rates at a time of weak economic growth and fragile business sentiment amid the war in Iran.

item.title: ‘We’re Traders, Not Nuns’: Embattled Brazil Hedge-Fund Plots Turnaround
item.description: Rogerio Xavier wants to set some things straight.
